articles.title: Rockets vs. Lakers: Lineups and Injury Updates (4/26)
articles.original_title: Houston Rockets vs. Los Angeles Lakers: lineups, injury reports (4/26) - Rockets Wire
articles.summary: The Houston Rockets and Los Angeles Lakers released their starting lineups and injury reports ahead of their April 26 matchup. Both teams listed key players as active, while noting a few questionable statuses due to minor ailments.
key_points: Rockets listed Jalen Green and Alperen Şengün in the starting five; Lakers started LeBron James, Anthony Davis, and D'Angelo Russell.
Los Angeles reported LeBron James as day‑to‑day with a sore ankle, while Houston listed a minor hamstring issue for Kevin Porter Jr.
Both teams indicated that the game would feature limited minutes for several bench players as coaches manage health ahead of the playoffs.

articles.title: Luka Dončić Injury Rumors Surface as Lakers Prepare to Return
articles.original_title: Surprising Luka Doncic Injury News Emerges As Lakers Return Looms - heavy.com
articles.summary: Heavy.com reported emerging rumors that Dallas Mavericks star Luka Dončić may be dealing with an undisclosed injury, coinciding with the Los Angeles Lakers' upcoming return to action. The article emphasized that the news is unconfirmed and that the Lakers are focused on their own roster health.
key_points: The report suggests Dončić could be sidelined for upcoming games, though no official statement has been released.
Lakers' preparation remains centered on LeBron James and Anthony Davis, with no direct impact from the Dončić rumor.
The article cautions readers that the injury news is speculative and should be treated as such until verified.

articles.title: Bronny James Gains Confidence After Scoring First Playoff Points
articles.original_title: Bronny James: Scoring First Playoff Points Provided Great Confidence
articles.summary: Bronny James recorded his first playoff points for the Los Angeles Lakers, hitting a three‑pointer and finishing a transition lob in Game 3. His contributions, though limited in minutes, have boosted his confidence and solidified his role in the postseason rotation.
key_points: Bronny scored a three‑pointer and added a layup on a fast‑break, marking his first playoff points.
His performance reflects growing trust from the coaching staff despite limited playing time.
The rookie’s confidence surge is viewed as a positive sign for the Lakers' depth heading into later rounds.

Statistical snapshots included shooting percentages, rebound totals, and assist counts for both teams.
Yahoo’s play‑by‑play allowed fans to follow the game's flow in real time, including time‑outs and foul trouble.

articles.title: How to Watch Lakers vs. Rockets Playoff Game 4 on April 26
articles.original_title: Where to watch Los Angeles Lakers vs Houston Rockets Playoffs: TV channel, start time, streaming for April 26 - USA Today
articles.summary: USA Today outlined the broadcast details for Game 4 of the Lakers‑Rockets playoff series, including TV channels, streaming options, and start time. The preview also highlighted two key storylines to watch during the matchup.
key_points: Game 4 will air on TNT at 8:30 p.m. ET, with streaming available via the TNT app, Max, and participating cable providers.
Key storylines: the Rockets' “Dream Shake” offense and the Lakers' effort to contain it while seeking a series‑tying win.
The article aggregates information from Sports Illustrated and other sources to help fans locate the broadcast.

articles.title: Lakers Assistant Coach Lindsey Harding Joins Prime Video as WNBA Analyst
articles.original_title: Lakers Assistant Coach Lindsey Harding Joining Prime Video As WNBA Studio Analyst
articles.summary: Lindsey Harding, a former player and current assistant coach for the Los Angeles Lakers, will transition to a media role as a studio analyst for Prime Video's WNBA coverage. Her move reflects head coach JJ Redick's strategy of assembling a staff that blends veteran experience with emerging coaching talent.
key_points: Harding leaves the Lakers' coaching staff to work as a WNBA studio analyst for Prime Video.
Her hiring aligns with JJ Redick's approach of mixing seasoned and younger coaches on his staff.
Harding brings playing experience and recent coaching insight to the broadcast booth.

articles.title: Byron Scott Explains 1987 Trade for Mychal Thompson
articles.original_title: "Not a knock on A.C. Green or Kurt Rambis" - Byron Scott reveals why the L.A. Lakers traded for Mychal Thompson in 1987 - Basketball Network
articles.summary: Former Lakers guard Byron Scott clarified that the 1987 trade for Mychal Thompson was not a criticism of teammates A.C. Green or Kurt Rambis, but a strategic move to add size and veteran leadership. The trade aimed to bolster the roster for a championship push during the Showtime era.
key_points: The trade was motivated by a need for additional front‑court size and experience.
Scott emphasized that the move was not a slight against Green or Rambis.
Mychal Thompson’s acquisition was part of the Lakers’ broader championship strategy in the late 1980s.

articles.title: Lakers Pull Off Historic Comeback to Beat Rockets in Game 3
articles.original_title: Lakers Achieved Rare NBA Playoffs History With Game 3 Comeback Vs. Rockets
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers rallied from a six‑point deficit with 34.4 seconds left to defeat the Houston Rockets, securing a rare playoff comeback. Marcus Smart and LeBron James combined for the decisive play that turned a likely loss into a win, marking a notable moment in NBA postseason history.
key_points: Lakers trailed 101‑95 with under 35 seconds remaining before the comeback.
Marcus Smart and LeBron James executed the critical sequence that clinched the victory.
The win represents an uncommon late‑game turnaround in NBA playoff lore.

articles.title: Rockets’ Game 3 Meltdown Becomes Historic Moment
articles.original_title: Rockets Make History in Game 3 Meltdown vs. Lakers - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: Houston’s Game 3 loss to the Los Angeles Lakers was marked by a dramatic collapse that entered the record books. The Rockets surrendered a lead in the final seconds, resulting in a historic turnaround that will be remembered for its rarity in playoff contexts.
key_points: The Rockets held a late lead before the Lakers’ comeback in the final minute.
The collapse is noted as a historic playoff meltdown for Houston.
The game highlights the volatility of momentum in postseason basketball.

articles.title: Marcus Smart Urges Lakers to ‘Be Like a Lion’ Ahead of Game 4
articles.original_title: Marcus Smart Implores Lakers To ‘Be Like A Lion’ In Game 4 Against Rockets
articles.summary: Marcus Smart, whose offseason signing has been praised for its defensive impact, delivered a motivational message to his teammates before Game 4 against the Rockets. He emphasized toughness and unity, echoing the lion motif as the Lakers seek to close the series.
key_points: Smart’s leadership is highlighted as a key factor in the Lakers’ playoff run.
He called on the team to embody the strength and ferocity of a lion.
The message came ahead of a pivotal Game 4 matchup with Houston.

articles.title: Victor Wembanyama cleared to play in Spurs‑Trail Blazers Game 4
articles.original_title: Victor Wembanyama cleared from concussion protocol to play in Spurs-Trail Blazers Game 4
articles.summary: Victor Wembanyama has been removed from the concussion protocol and is eligible for Game 4 against Portland. The Spurs lost Game 2 when he was injured, but secured a win in Game 3 while he watched from the bench.
key_points: Wembanyama cleared to play after concussion protocol.
Spurs lost Game 2 due to his injury.
Spurs won Game 3 in Portland with Wembanyama in street clothes.

key_points: Prop projections focus on Boston’s point guard duo.
Payton Pritchard projected to exceed his usual scoring/assist totals.
Models suggest high-scoring potential for both teams’ guards.

articles.title: Donte DiVincenzo joins list of No. 0 jersey players with Achilles tears
articles.original_title: Donte DiVincenzo becomes 4th player with No. 0 jersey to tear his Achilles in the past year
articles.summary: Donte DiVincenzo suffered an Achilles rupture, becoming the fourth player wearing the No. 0 jersey to experience that injury in the past year. The other players are Tyrese Haliburton, Damian Lillard and Jayson Tatum, all of whom share the same jersey number and recent injury type.
key_points: DiVincenzo’s Achilles tear adds to a growing list of No. 0 jersey injuries.
Tyrese Haliburton, Damian Lillard and Jayson Tatum also suffered Achilles ruptures this year.
The pattern highlights a concerning injury trend among players wearing No. 0.

key_points: Series tied 2‑2, making Game 4 decisive.
Parlay includes bets on points, rebounds and total score.
Analysts anticipate a close, low‑margin game.

articles.title: Playoff preview: Nuggets face elimination, Thunder eye sweep, Pistons in trouble
articles.original_title: Monday's NBA playoffs: Nuggets face elimination, Thunder could sweep, and Pistons in trouble?
articles.summary: The article reviews the current state of the NBA playoffs, highlighting Denver’s fight to avoid elimination, Oklahoma City’s chance to sweep their opponent, and Detroit’s precarious position. It also references the recent rivalry between the Minnesota Timberwolves and the Nuggets, who have met three times in the past four postseason years.
key_points: Nuggets must win to stay alive in their series.
Thunder could complete a sweep in their matchup.
Timberwolves‑Nuggets rivalry has produced three playoff meetings in four years.

articles.title: Nuggets‑Timberwolves Series Becomes a War of Attrition
articles.original_title: Nuggets-Timberwolves series is a war of attrition — and both teams are losing the battle
articles.summary: The first-round matchup between Denver and Minnesota has turned into a grueling battle, with both teams exhausting key players and resources. As the series progresses, the eventual winner is likely to emerge with limited stamina to challenge the Western Conference's elite teams.
key_points: Both squads are experiencing fatigue and injuries after a physically demanding series.
The winner will have little left in the tank for the next round against top Western contenders.
Coaches are adjusting rotations to preserve health while still trying to secure a win.

articles.title: Cavaliers Aim to Extend Lead in Game 4 vs. Raptors
articles.original_title: Cavs at Raptors Game 4 open gamethread
articles.summary: Cleveland enters Game 4 of the Eastern Conference semifinals looking to push its series advantage to 3‑1 against Toronto. The game thread highlights the Cavs' strategy to maintain momentum and close out the series early.
key_points: Cavs seek a third consecutive win to put the Raptors on the brink of elimination.
Key players such as Darius Garland and Donovan Mitchell are expected to lead the offensive push.
Toronto will need a strong defensive effort to stay alive in the series.

Betting lines favor the Spurs, but the projection highlights a potential upset factor from Portland’s guard.
Other prop picks include elevated rebound and assist totals for both teams' primary playmakers.

articles.title: Knicks Edge Hawks 114‑98 as Karl‑Anthony Towns Shines
articles.original_title: Knicks 114, Hawks 98: “KAT was fantastic”
articles.summary: New York defeats Atlanta 114‑98, with Karl‑Anthony Towns delivering a standout performance that earned praise from the team. The win leaves the Knicks tied in the series, maintaining the competitive balance as the matchup heads to the next game.
key_points: Towns posted a strong stat line, contributing significantly on both ends of the floor.
The Knicks’ defense limited the Hawks to under 100 points, showcasing improved cohesion.
The series remains dead‑locked, setting up a pivotal Game 5.
